description: Introduction of Phase 2 to the trine-eval Self-Upgrade spec. Phase 1 (Sprints 1-6) closes 15 gaps against Anthropic's eval-driven-development playbook within existing project-type rubrics. Phase 2 expands the harness's mission to support a new project-type domain ('harness-build' / agent runtime harnesses), sourced from an externally-supplied playbook brought in by the user. This is a Method-axis scope expansion: the product vision in spec.md is being widened to add a new project-type vertical. Phase 2 also entails downstream Material-axis records (new rubric file, traps catalog) which will be classified as scheduled and suppressed when they land within Phase 2 sprint scope.

evidence.claim: Sprint 6 is the most recently active sprint and its declared scope was JIT context retrieval patterns only — Phase 2 / harness-build / agent-runtime rubric are not in its features array.

Pre-Phase-2 spec.md product vision was bounded to 'close 15 identified gaps against the playbook' (Anthropic eval-driven-dev playbook). It did not reference agent-runtime harnesses, harness-build, or any new project-type vertical.
evidence.verification: git show HEAD~1:.harness/spec.md | grep -i 'agent-runtime\|harness-build' || echo 'absent in prior revision'

evidence.claim: The eval-rubric registry currently lists five rubrics and does not include an agent-runtime-harness / harness-build rubric.
The change is user-initiated and deliberate: user explicitly supplied an external playbook document and asked to plan integration sprints. This satisfies the active (henkoten) criterion.
Phase 1 telemetry shows uniform PASS with no failure pattern that would force a domain pivot — corroborating that Phase 2 is additive expansion, not corrective response.

Suppression rule does not apply: Sprint 6 scope (JIT context retrieval) does not include spec.md expansion, rubric-registry expansion, or harness-build project-type work. Phase 2 introduction is therefore unscheduled relative to the active sprint, even though the user intentionally initiated it. Active+unscheduled is a valid combination.

andon_signal.reason: Pre-amendment: spec.md vision and feature list did not authorize Phase 2 work, risking orphaned sprints. Resolved by spec.md amendment + features.json bootstrap before Sprint 7 begins.
evidence: .harness/features.json (was absent — created as part of Phase 2 prep)

resolution_summary: Spec amended with Phase 1 / Phase 2 sections. features.json bootstrapped with 19 entries (16 implemented + 3 planned). Sprints 7-11 appended to sprints.json with council-recommended fixes folded in (unconditional Sprint 7 threshold; Should-NOT gates for prior-deliverable protection; ephemeral Sprint 10; templates/by-rubric/ layout for Sprint 8; playbook_stage field scoped to harness-build mode in Sprint 9).

description: Behavioral-coverage rule was introduced into the contract/grading methodology outside any sprint's declared scope. The 2-way grader split (deterministic/llm-judge) has been replaced with a 3-way split (behavioral/structural/llm-judge), and a new constraint requires behavioral criteria to hold >=60% of total weight unless the contract's Technical Notes justify the exception. Affected files have been edited but not committed at the time of detection. Sprint 7 (harness-build rubric) will inherit this rule as soon as the Generator reads the modified agents/skills. The rule is coherent and intentional per the user's auto-memory dated 2026-05-16, but the rollout bypassed sprint negotiation and council review.

evidence.claim: Seven plugin agent/skill files have uncommitted edits at the moment Sprint 7 begins.
The diffs introduce a 3-way grader split (behavioral/structural/llm-judge) and a >=60% behavioral-coverage requirement, replacing the prior 2-way (deterministic/llm-judge) scheme.
Sprint 6 closed with status PASS and did not include behavioral-coverage rule work in its features array; the rule predates this autorun and falls outside any negotiated sprint contract.
The change is consonant with the harness's mission (eval-driven development) and aligns with Sprint 7's emphasis on behavioral verification for harness-build rubrics; risk of distortion is low.

The user's auto-memory notes the rule was decided on 2026-05-16 ('trine-eval contracts now require >=60% behavioral-criterion weight; 3-way grader split (behavioral/structural/llm-judge) replaces old 2-way'), corroborating that the change is intentional rather than accidental drift.

resolution_summary: Ratified via DEC-0007 (2026-06-01T14:24:00Z) — single-prompt L4 path under nemawashi_walkthrough_version: 0. Sprint 9 schema port (commit 98cfa49) brought the tasks.json schema into conformance with the 3-way grader split, completing the rule downstream into the in-repo audit chain.

yokoten.adaptation_notes: All contracts MUST use the 3-way grader_type enum (behavioral | structural | llm-judge) and MUST target >=60% behavioral coverage per DEC-0007. The cached v0.3.3 2-way enum (deterministic | llm-judge) is deprecated and MUST NOT appear in new contract authoring. When the natural behavioral surface is limited (doc-heavy sprints), refer to PROC-002 for the coverage-justification standard; the 60% floor is a hard rule, not a target.

description: Sprints 7, 8, and 9 ran the full /trine-eval:harness-sprint loop end-to-end without invoking the council fan-out documented in `.council/config.json` as `review_frequency: every-sprint`. Sprint 7 has a sprint-prebrief file; Sprint 8 and Sprint 9 do not. No post-sprint course-corrections file exists in `.council/course-corrections/` for any of Sprints 7-9. The Phase 1.5 plan insertion (DEC pending) was authored by the orchestrator in a /trine-eval:harness-sprint context, not via a council position paper + nemawashi. This is a Method-axis drift: the stated cadence is per-sprint, observed cadence is per-three-sprints (and only when the user manually invokes /henkaten-council:council-autorun). The risk is corrective lag: scope-guardian and architect findings about sprints 7-9 have not been recorded, and any drift that occurred has accumulated unaudited across three sprints.

evidence.claim: review_frequency in .council/config.json is set to every-sprint.
Only sprint-07.json exists in .council/sprint-prebrief/; sprint-08.json and sprint-09.json are absent.
decision-log.jsonl contains 7 DEC entries, all from sprint-context 0, 6, or 7; no entries for sprint-context 8 or sprint-context 9.
Sprints 7, 8, 9 all completed PASS at 100% weighted, so the drift was not surfaced by a sprint failure that would otherwise force council attention.

The Phase 1.5 plan amendment (Sprint 9 insertion, sprints 9-11 renumbered to 10-12) was decided unilaterally by the orchestrator within a /trine-eval:harness-sprint context, not via a council position paper. Reversibility is reversible (git revert of 497f184), but the change is structurally similar to DEC-0002 (spec amendment) and DEC-0004 (sprint reordering) which both went through nemawashi-equivalent ratification.

resolution_summary: Sprint 10 ran with full council fan-out: sprint-prebrief/sprint-10.json authored pre-sprint (Cynefin classified complicated, conf 0.75); after-sprint-10.md course-corrections file written by this fan-out; all four council agents (architect, scope-guardian, henkaten-detector, retrospective-mini) produced structured reviews with verification spot-checks. Structural closure via DEC-0010 (Sprint 13 plan amendment) + DEC-0011 (bidirectional council-check-gates extension, linked HK-0005) — Sprint 13 will install the b2 post-sprint auto-trigger, removing manual-cadence dependence by construction. Promotion to resolved deferred until Sprint 13 lands and at least two subsequent sprints exhibit the auto-trigger working end-to-end (per Henkaten Detector recommendation 2026-06-01). RECURRENCE 2026-06-02: Sprint 11 ran without pre-sprint council fan-out (sprint-prebrief authored post-hoc per HK-0003 yokoten retroactive-closure rule). Status reverted responded -> classified per HK-0008 recurrence evidence and DEC-0013 ratification. Resolved-promotion requires Sprint 13 b2 auto-trigger to land AND at least two subsequent sprints exhibiting the auto-trigger working end-to-end.

yokoten.adaptation_notes: Every-sprint review_frequency means literally every sprint. The Sprint 13 b2 auto-trigger eliminates manual-cadence dependence by construction once it lands; during any window where the auto-trigger is disabled, broken, or pre-implementation, the orchestrator MUST manually invoke /henkaten-council:council-autorun for each sprint. Missed cadence requires retroactive closure (post-hoc sprint-prebrief, post-hoc course-corrections, post-hoc mini retro) before the next sprint begins. HK-0003 (Sprints 7-9 bypass; Sprint 10 manual recovery; Sprint 13 structural fix) is the canonical reference for this pattern.

description: The in-repo plugin source `plugins/trine-eval/skills/sprint-workflow/SKILL.md` documents only Steps 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 — it does not contain Step 0.5 (regression gate), Step 1d (tasks.json emission), or Step 3d (Batch API submission grouping). The Sprint 9 schema port added prose in `plugins/trine-eval/skills/sprint-contract/SKILL.md` at lines 74, 140, 142 referencing these three steps as if they exist in the in-repo workflow. The references resolve only against the cached/published v0.3.3 plugin from which actual harness-sprint execution loads — the in-repo source has silently diverged from the published artifact. Architect and scope-guardian independently confirmed the gap; orchestrator spot-checked via direct grep (logged to audit-log). Andon issued `alert` (not `stop`) because runtime execution against the cached SKILL.md is unaffected; the divergence is documentation correctness, not execution risk. This Henkaten captures the structural discovery: the repo-source workflow has been drifting from the published workflow without registration, and Sprint 9's port now creates dangling references that make the drift visible.

evidence.claim: In-repo sprint-workflow/SKILL.md defines exactly six steps (0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5) — no Step 0.5, no Step 1d, no Step 3d.
The Sprint 9 schema port wrote three references to non-existent in-repo workflow steps into sprint-contract/SKILL.md at lines 74, 140, 142.
The reference points to a published v0.3.3 cached harness-sprint/SKILL.md that the harness execution path uses — the in-repo source has not been ported to parity.
Sprint 9's declared Out-of-Scope forbids touching any SKILL.md other than sprint-contract/SKILL.md, so the gap cannot be closed inside Sprint 9 — it requires a follow-up scheduled sprint.

Architect (this swarm) issued andon `alert` (not `stop`) because the actual workflow execution loads from cached v0.3.3 SKILL.md which has the steps. Scope-guardian and henkaten-detector concurred. Orchestrator spot-checked the underlying grep claim and logged PASS to audit-log.

Classified as change_origin=passive because the gap has been present continuously since the v0.3.3 / repo-source divergence began; Sprint 9 did not introduce the gap, only surfaced it through new prose that references the missing steps.

andon_signal.reason: Documentation references in-repo workflow steps that do not exist in the in-repo workflow file; runtime execution against the cached v0.3.3 SKILL.md is unaffected, so this is documentation correctness, not execution risk. Resolved within takt budget — all three council agents (architect, scope-guardian, henkaten-detector) concurred alert is the correct level.

description: Post-Sprint-10 in-session plan amendment (commit 71ce8e7) extended Sprint 13's `council-check-gate` feature into a bidirectional `council-check-gates` feature set, adding a Step-5 post-sprint auto-trigger that invokes /henkaten-council:council-autorun automatically when config.governance.review_frequency is 'every-sprint'. The amendment edits .harness/sprints.json (Sprint 13 entry features array + notes block) and .harness/spec.md (Phase 1.6 section). User-authorized in-session 2026-06-01 ('go ahead with option 1'). Lives outside Sprint 10's declared scope but is the structural closure mechanism for HK-0003 — auto-trigger removes manual-cadence drift by construction. Response-type log-only because the change is already ratified via DEC-0011 in the same session.

evidence.claim: Sprint 10's declared sole deliverable is plugins/trine-eval/agents/planner.md; Out-of-Scope explicitly excludes touching prior contracts and forbids hook/config/SKILL.md changes outside planner.md.
evidence.verification: grep -nE 'sole deliverable|Modifying any prior sprint|Adding new hooks' .harness/contracts/sprint-10.md

evidence.claim: Sprint 13's notes block now contains the post-Sprint-10 extension language describing the bidirectional gates (b1 pre-sprint + b2 post-sprint auto-trigger).
evidence.verification: grep -cE 'bidirectional council-check gates|post-sprint auto-trigger|post-Sprint-10 per user authorization' .harness/sprints.json

evidence.claim: Phase 1.6 prose in spec.md was rewritten to declare the bidirectional gates and attribute the extension to 'Architect P-05, extended post-Sprint-10'.
Change is fully reversible (git revert 71ce8e7) and impacts only a forward-looking sprint plan (Sprint 13 has not begun); no executed work is invalidated.
Active origin: user explicitly approved the option in-session before the commit, mirroring the DEC-0010 pre-Sprint-10 fan-out pattern.

resolution_summary: Ratified via DEC-0011 (composite audit-chain entry) on 2026-06-01. The extension is the structural closure mechanism for HK-0003: Sprint 13 deliverable (b2) auto-triggers the council fan-out post-sprint, removing the manual-cadence dependency that allowed Sprints 7-9 bypass. Linked to HK-0003 transition (classified → responded) in the same DEC entry.

yokoten.adaptation_notes: Post-completion in-session plan amendments are valid and reversible, but they REQUIRE a same-session DEC entry capturing the user authorization to maintain audit-chain wholeness. The 71ce8e7 / DEC-0011 sequence is the canonical example: scope expansion of a forward-looking sprint plan was user-authorized in-session, then captured by a DEC entry in the immediate-next council fan-out. The 'capture in same session' part is load-bearing -- without it, the change shape-matches HK-0003's documented failure mode (unilateral orchestrator-initiated plan amendment outside council review).

description: Generator running inside /trine-eval:harness-sprint 11 could not dispatch the Planner subagent into the ephemeral tmp directory to execute the harness-kickoff workflow end-to-end, and instead authored the expected spec.md and sprints.json directly per planner.md's Harness-Build Mode section. Disclosed in dogfood-findings.md Section 1 line 30: 'Planner subagent dispatch was not available to the Generator in this session — the Generator authored the expected output per planner.md's Harness-Build Mode section.' This is a runtime capability limit of the harness's meta-dispatch surface (a Generator subagent cannot itself spawn a Planner subagent in a sibling tmp working directory), not a documentation-only gap. Distinct from HK-0004 (documentation gap in sprint-workflow/SKILL.md missing Steps 0.5/1d/3d): HK-0004 is missing-documented-steps; HK-0006 is a runtime nest-dispatch limit affecting ephemeral-fixture sprints specifically. Remediation options: (i) explicit ephemeral-fixture workflow variant that pre-runs the kickoff via the top-level user session, (ii) documented PARTIAL-verdict path when nested dispatch is unavailable with a side-effect observable as evidence anchor, (iii) workflow-level subagent_dispatch.jsonl audit observable. Architect P-A3 routes (iii) to Sprint 13 deliverable (e); deferred to Sprint 13 contract drafting.

evidence.claim: dogfood-findings.md Section 1 explicitly discloses the subagent-dispatch unavailability
evidence.verification: grep -n 'Planner subagent dispatch was not available' .harness/dogfood-findings.md

evidence.claim: Sprint 11 eval verdict PARTIAL roots both J10 and J11 failures in synthetic authoring
Gap is distinct from HK-0004 (documentation gap) — HK-0006 is a runtime nest-dispatch limit
change_origin=passive because no Sprint-11 edit introduced the limitation; the inability surfaced when Sprint 11 was the first sprint requiring nested kickoff dispatch

description: Sprint 11's sprints.json declares estimated_complexity='medium', but the post-hoc sprint-prebrief/sprint-11.json retroactively classifies Cynefin as 'complex' with confidence 0.4 (probe-sense-respond), and the rationale openly states 'cause-effect was NOT knowable in advance' and 'the sprint's purpose was specifically to discover what would happen — not to confirm a hypothesis.' The gap is a measurement-criteria misalignment: the estimated_complexity field on sprints.json (Phase 1 vocabulary: low/medium/high) does not encode Cynefin domain, and the council prebrief vocabulary (clear/complicated/complex/chaotic) is a different ontology. Sprint 11 is the first sprint where the two vocabularies materially disagree (Sprint 10 prebrief was complicated/0.75 against medium — closer alignment). Relevant to SW-CAND-E (classification reclassification protocol via loop_shapes[]) which was deferred at DEC-0012 pending more evidence; Sprint 11 may constitute the second data point.

evidence.claim: sprints.json Sprint 11 estimated_complexity is 'medium'
sprint-prebrief/sprint-11.json cynefin classification is 'complex' with confidence 0.4
sprint-prebrief rationale explicitly admits non-knowability of cause-effect, which is the canonical complex-domain signal
the two vocabularies are different ontologies — estimated_complexity is t-shirt sizing; Cynefin is decision-domain

description: HK-0003 (Sprints 7-9 council bypass) was promoted to 'responded' after Sprint 10 ran with the full pre-sprint council fan-out (sprint-prebrief/sprint-10.json authored at sprint entry; post-sprint course-corrections/after-sprint-10.md). The promotion-to-resolved was explicitly deferred per Henkaten Detector recommendation until 'Sprint 13 lands and at least two subsequent sprints exhibit the auto-trigger working end-to-end'. Sprint 11 did NOT exhibit the pattern: sprint-prebrief/sprint-11.json exists but is dated 2026-06-02T11:00:00Z (post-sprint, retroactive — its own rationale field declares 'this prebrief is written post-sprint per the HK-0003 yokoten retroactive-closure rule'); no course-corrections/after-sprint-11.md existed at the time this candidate was raised; no audit-log entry shows a pre-sprint-11 council fan-out. The HK-0003 pattern is therefore actively recurring — Sprint 10 was a one-off manual-recovery sprint, not the start of a sustained cadence. The structural closure (Sprint 13 b2 auto-trigger) has not yet landed. HK-0008 is the recurrence evidence record linked to HK-0003's status revert from 'responded' back to 'classified'. Also subsumes evidence for yokoten obligations un-discharged (HK-0002/HK-0003/HK-0005 yokoten blocks not surfaced at Sprint 11 entry).

evidence.claim: sprint-prebrief/sprint-11.json is self-declared retroactive
Sprint 11 ran end-to-end (status partial, weighted 76) without any pre-sprint council artifact
HK-0003 resolution_summary explicitly defers resolved-promotion until at least two subsequent sprints exhibit the auto-trigger; Sprint 11 does not exhibit it
Sprint 13 (which contains the b2 auto-trigger deliverable) has not yet executed
HK-0002/HK-0003/HK-0005 carry yokoten.applicable_to_subsequent_sprints=['all']; none surfaced at Sprint 11 entry

description: Sprint 10 retrospective flagged a downward trend in behavioral coverage toward the 60% floor: S8=66%, S9=63%, S10=62%. Sprint 11 continues at 62%, sustaining the floor-grazing pattern that PROC-002 (ratified in DEC-0012) was designed to surface. Sprint 11's contract DID include the Coverage Justification — the Technical Notes 'Behavioral coverage (≥ 60% target)' subsection meets PROC-002 substantively — but the persistent floor-grazing is itself a Method-axis informational signal that the contract-drafting process is cost-minimizing toward the rule's hard edge. Four consecutive Phase-2 sprints at or near 62% qualifies as an inferred pattern observation. Stable-at-floor (not still descending) means coverage discipline is met by minimum compliance, not surplus. Per Sprint 10 retrospective Pattern Observation 2, flagged for the next pdca cycle. HK-0009 is the formal Henkaten record so the pattern accumulates a distinct ID for cross-sprint reference.

evidence.claim: Sprint 11 grader split is behavioral=62%, structural=14%, llm_judge=24%
Prior-sprint trend S8=66, S9=63, S10=62, S11=62
PROC-002 was satisfied substantively — Sprint 11 contract Technical Notes includes the Coverage Justification content
the four-sprint pattern of floor-grazing is consistent with cost-minimization behavior PROC-002 was designed to surface, not eliminate

description: Sprint 12 counter-evidence to HK-0008 recurrence: .council/sprint-prebrief/sprint-12.json was authored 2026-06-02T11:45:00Z (pre-implementation; bc14823 first implementation commit dated later), with no retroactive-closure disclaimer in the file. Sprint 12 exhibited the intended pre-sprint council fan-out shape that HK-0008/HK-0003 require, breaking the Sprint-11 retroactive pattern. CAVEAT: Sprint 12 is operator-driven, not yet structurally auto-triggered — the Sprint 13 b2 deliverable (post-sprint auto-trigger in workflow Step 5) has not yet landed. Per HK-0003 resolution_summary, Sprint 12 cannot count toward the '>=2 subsequent sprints exhibiting the auto-trigger working end-to-end' criterion because the structural auto-trigger does not yet exist; it counts only as behavioral conformance to the manual yokoten obligation tracked by HK-0008. Recommendation: hold HK-0003 at classified (do NOT promote to resolved); HK-0008 status updated from 'classified' to 'responded' on the basis of Sprint 12 breaking the single-occurrence (Sprint 11) recurrence pattern. Promote both to 'resolved' once auto-trigger demonstrates end-to-end success on two consecutive post-S13 sprints.

evidence.claim: Sprint 12 prebrief authored pre-implementation, no retroactive-closure disclaimer
Sprint 12 prebrief timestamp precedes first implementation commit bc14823
Sprint 13 b2 auto-trigger has not yet been implemented (Sprint 13 not started)
HK-0008 promoted from classified -> responded (single-occurrence pattern, broken by Sprint 12)

resolution_summary: HK-0008 (Sprint 11 council-bypass recurrence) responded — Sprint 12 broke the Sprint-11 retroactive pattern by authoring pre-sprint prebrief pre-implementation. Promotion to 'resolved' deferred until Sprint 13 b2 (structural auto-trigger) lands AND ≥2 subsequent sprints exhibit the auto-trigger end-to-end (joint criterion with HK-0003).

description: HK-0009 (behavioral-coverage floor-grazing pattern) UPDATED with Sprint 12 evidence and IMPACT-LEVEL PROMOTION informational -> actionable. Five-sprint trend now: S8=66%, S9=63%, S10=62%, S11=62%, S12=54%. Sprint 12 is the first Phase-2 sprint to drop BELOW the 60% floor — the static-artifact carve-out (per sprint-contract SKILL.md) was load-bearing for contract approval. This is a qualitative state change (descending-through-floor, not stable-at-floor). The carve-out is legitimate and was explicitly invoked in Sprint 12 Technical Notes citing the Sprint 7 precedent, but Sprint 12 is only the second Phase-2 sprint to invoke it (Sprint 7 was the first), confirming the cost-minimization signal HK-0009 named is empirically deepening. Additionally, Sprint 12's Evaluator review flagged B1 ('simulation behavioral') as 'formally an LLM-judge criterion dressed in behavioral language' — without B1's 14% weight, behavioral coverage is 40%. Recommendation: Cycle 3 PDCA action item to (i) codify when the static-artifact carve-out is permitted; (ii) consider retiring 'simulation behavioral' as a grader-type label (subsumed into structural or llm-judge); (iii) introduce a non-fakeable behavioral observable for sprints that today rely on simulation behavioral. Evidence base now Sprints 7/11/12 for the carve-out and B1-classification pattern.

evidence.claim: Sprint 12 grader_split.behavioral_pct = 54% (below 60% floor)
Sprint 12 contract Technical Notes explicitly invoke the static-artifact carve-out and cite Sprint 7 precedent
Sprint 12 Evaluator review flagged B1 as formally LLM-judge
Without B1, behavioral coverage drops from 54% to 40%
evidence.verification: grep -n 'reclassified as structural\|drops behavioral coverage\|drops to 40%' .harness/contracts/sprint-12.md
